text: Entering this space, my initial sensation is one of curiosity blended with slight unease. The sterile brightness, suspended objects, and geometric transparency of glass feel both clinical and enigmatic. The clear sightlines into another world behind glass evoke the threshold between observer and participant. I am reminded of how art and research environments often invite me to question the boundaries of knowledge, boundaries as transparent yet formidable as these walls. Here, I sense both anticipation and detachment—a liminal pause before engaging further.
